item.title: Worth it
item.dc:creator: huntsnhava40

item.title: Big day for a little Puckett
item.description:
Hi everyone, I'm MrPuckett
Today is a very special day in my home - It's my puppy PJ's 2nd birthday!!!
I adopted him from Nashville Humane at only 3 months old and I severely underestimated how tough raising a puppy was. Those first few months tested my patience, and there were many days where I wondered if I made a mistake.
But as he got older the vet allowed more and more exercise
And I tried to expose him to as many environments as I could
He rode in that stroller all over Nashville until he was old enough to run, and then accomplished some pretty amazing things
At only one year of age, PJ ran his first two marathons, over 40 different half marathons, and...
Over 2,000 miles in one year!!!
An insane year for my little 12lb buddy who loves nothing more than 3 hour runs and 3 hour cuddle sessions. He has grown into the most loving and incredible friend over the last year.
Yesterday we celebrated Nanna's 16th birthday as we kicked off Puckett Week
7 days of fundraising and a Supply Drive for domestic violence survivors facing homelessness. Look at how adorable @carlsaganforlife made little PJ, I love this image
Domestic violence is the leading cause of homelessness among women and children in America. Last year on Imgur we raised over $30,000 worth of donations and goods for survivors!
Many survivors of abuse must face the terrifying decision of staying with their abuser or taking their kids or dogs to a homeless shelter. The Mary Parrish Center provides free housing, therapy, food and more to survivors - everything they might need to stay safe and start their life fresh again.
Continuing the week of Nanna's 16th birthday and PJ's 2nd, I thought it would be nice if anyone who can afford it donate $16 to The Mary Parrish Center. I also had them set up an Amazon Wishlist if you'd like to send anything directly to the survivors in their new safe apartment.
Adding PJ to the family was really tough at first, but I can't imagine being in a scenario where leaving an abuser could land us all homeless. This week we can make the transition from abused and homeless to housed and loved just a little bit easier for domestic violence survivors. Happy birthday PJ and Nanna, let's do some good in your names this week. ... To donate $16 - https://www.maryparrish.org/donate ... If you'd like to send goods to the survivors of domestic violence at Mary Parrish - https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/3USHUHIVSINQE?ref_=wl_share ... Thank you for reading my post, I'll see everyone again tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.dc:creator: evaunit117

item.title: Designed a bit of a daring dress for V-Day!
item.description:
Does this look deceptively simple? Yeah, I guess it does a bit. This was a little bit of a challenge to myself to work my patternmatching skills. I drafted this pattern using my bodice block. The bodice pattern just has 3 pieces—the neckline, the side front and the side back! The skirt is 4x quarter circle pieces to create those sweet chevrons.
The amazing thing about the bodice is that the neckline pattern piece is one straight, simple piece that goes from front to back (mirrored on both sides and matched down the middle). So there is no shoulder seam! The challenge here was preventing it from gaping and connecting the two sides at an angle that wasn’t too far off the shoulders and not cut too low in the front or the back. Geometry gone wild, y’all.
The back v is low but sits just above my bra which I’m really happy about because I’ve never been a fan of designs where you have to be braless for it to work. The invisible zipper was super challenging to match stripes on both sides. I kind of wish I painted the pull red with a little nail polish but it’s fine for now. My secret to a good zipper? Fusible hem tape to act as interfacing. Interfacing sucks and the hem tape works SO much better, my god. Anyways, I hemmed the skirt with a bias tape facing (AFTER trimming a few days after hanging it). Always gotta hang your bias skirts before hemming, don’t skip that.
The neckline on the front is pretty daring for me but I’m glad the design didn’t force it to be even lower than that! I think on the next iteration if I make this again, I will pull up the shoulders about a centimetre to firm up the neckline a little. We’re all learning as we go, nothing is ever as perfect as it might look!
As you can see a bit better from this picture, the skirt is made up of 4 pieces and is pattern matched along the front, back, and even the side seams. I just love the chevrons! Definitely planning a rainbow pride dress using this pattern one day when I can afford 5 more metres of cotton drill fabric. I sewed this over just about 4 livestreams, including the test run! I stream my work 4 times per week, a lot of patternmaking and some cross stitch and miscellaneous stuff thrown in. If you wanna watch me sewing stuff live, here is a shameless plug: http://twitch.tv/carbonchic
item.dc:creator: CarbonChic

item.title: You a sneaky snake Alabama.
item.description:
Hmm... that's not confusing at all huh. Wonder what the AL Secretary of State has to say about that.
Oh ok.
Good question. Maybe the original poster didn't provide enough context. Let's look at the entire mailer. P.S. what a snowflake that Merrill fella is.
Yea idk. Still kinda confusing. I'm capable of reading all the fine print but the BOLD arrow and text seems like it could make people think they needed to fill out and return the form. Pretty underhanded if you ask me. Wonder where the SOS gets the motivation from.
Oh ffs. This guy?!? Mr. "find me eleven thousand votes" guy? Man I tell ya the GOP got the creating solutions for problems that don't exist thing down. They must've read a book or took a class or something.
Oh good lord, shoot me now. Article: https://whnt.com/news/what-did-alabamas-top-election-official-learn-from-monitoring-russian-election/
item.dc:creator: telecomteardown

item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Valentine's Day Weekend story!
item.description:
Hoo-boy, this is going to go on the books as "the biggest waste of time." Let's get the story down. TLDR warning: if you don't want to read the story, jus read the memes.
Ok, so. Hotel room reserved, car topped up for oil change & fresh tires, & he had taken 2 days off work so we could spend the weekend together! WOO! ... except... as soon as he got here, his face was buried in his phone all thru our first dinner... This does not bode well.
It started bad, & just went downhill from there. First night at the hotel, he wasn't even remotely interested, & was far more interested in watching TV until 10pm, then playing video games until 1am, before heading to bed. "Ok, he just drove a long journey, there's always Sunday!" ... and then....
Once. A single time for the whole 4-day weekend. Not even heavy petting the rest of the time. Just... a single encounter for the whole weekend, & he was back to his TV & video games. Really says something about my sex life that the highlight of the whole weekend was watching the Halftime Show...
And then he drops this little factoid in my lap right before we were set to leave the hotel. So, I take time off work for the weekend, take the time & energy to shave my legs (bc, ladies, let's be honest: in the winter in cold areas, no one bothers to shave your legs unless you have a legit reason/excuse), & pack all the cute clothes for dates & whatnot... And then it turns out I was being used to help him cheat on his partner. If it wasn't for the fact I'm so thoroughly disappointed in his performance/visit, I might actually be mad. Now, to look up his partner & decide if I want to tell them where he really was this weekend. Bc he allegedly told them he was going somewhere else. See, this is why so many of us have trust issues. That & oatmeal raisin cookies.
Cat tax. Yes, his collar edge is left long on purpose. It's a Sentry Comfort Collar, & he yanks it off if you cut the tail off. So I leave it long, just to keep him from getting irritated & pulling it off. TLDR: just read the memes, like I said at the top. MV edit: Well, so glad to know that my disgustingly depressing sex life is entertaining. I would like to personally thank the guys who have made sure I got this far, but CBA. Be excellent to each other, folks. And quit wasting time on guys with more dick in their personality than in their pants. MV edit part 2: Look, I keep getting so much flack for my oatmeal raisin cookie comment. I ain't got a problem with oatmeal. Or raisins. I just don't like cooked raisins. & I especially don't like biting into a cooked raisin, after being told it was chocolate.
item.dc:creator: SerenePrincess013

item.dc:creator: Guesswholikesyou

item.title: Now I gotta beat the 50/50 in 5 years... but we're getting there.
item.description: Last Valentine's Day sucked. Dying sucked too. But as of 1123 PM last night I made it through the year that was supposed to kill me. The struggling to breathe, the inability to walk, take a shower... it really sucked. The pain of learning to stand, learning to wheel around... walking? It all sucked. Every moment was a new learned pain, and a little triumph knowing that as long as I hurt? I was beating the alternative. And so I sit here, listening to the Mountain Goats This Year, wondering if the luck will keep going. Still stuck in a chair for periods, progress is slow and steady but it's winning the race. Thanks for those who have kept in contact and checking on me. I hope all is well for you and yours, and that you have a great day! Dovie'andi se tovya sagain, -The Librarian
item.dc:creator: MidnightLibrarian
